nodes.x: I am the rose of Sharon, and the lily of the valleys.
And Sharon shall be a fold of flocks, and the valley of Achor a place for the herds to lie down in, for my people that have sought me.
As the lily among thorns, so is my love among the daughters.

Previous to our coming to these valleys, I wished and prayed, that when we went to the valleys, there would not any of the wicked persons follow us, who are eternally hanging on our skirts. These arc my feelings and desires now, and the earnest wishes of hundreds and thousands of men and women who dwell in these valleys.
As the lily among thorns, so is my love among the daughters.
I am the rose of Sharon, and the lily of the valleys.
I am my beloved’s, and my beloved is mine: he feedeth among the lilies.
And that which fell among thorns are they, which, when they have heard, go forth, and are choked with cares and riches and pleasures of this life, and bring no fruit to perfection.
¶ My beloved is mine, and I am his: he feedeth among the lilies.
And her daughters which are in the field shall be slain by the sword; and they shall know that I am the Lord.
As the apple tree among the trees of the wood, so is my beloved among the sons. I sat down under his shadow with great delight, and his fruit was sweet to my taste.

"The last time I ever saw him he related to me the following vision which he had in his field in open day: *I was carried away in a vision and found myself in the midst of a vast orchard of fruit trees. I became hungry and wandered through this vast orchard searching for fruit to eat, but I found none. While I stood in amazement finding no fruit in the midst of so many trees, they began to fall…
And it came to pass that I beheld a tree, whose fruit was desirable to make one happy.

And as I partook of the fruit thereof it filled my soul with exceedingly great joy; wherefore, I began to be desirous that my family should partake of it also; for I knew that it was desirable above all other fruit.
And as I cast my eyes round about, that perhaps I might discover my family also, I beheld a river of water; and it ran along, and it was near the tree of which I was partaking the fruit.
He brought me to the banqueting house, and his banner over me was love.

He received me with a hearty welcome, and with that frank and
kind manner so universal with him in after years.
He brought me forth also into a large place; he delivered me, because he delighted in me.
He hath filled me with his love, even unto the consuming of my flesh.
Then I was by him, as one brought up with him: and I was daily his delight, rejoicing always before him;
He brought me forth also into a large place: he delivered me, because he delighted in me.
Stay me with flagons, comfort me with apples: for I am sick of love.
Keep me as the apple of the eye, hide me under the shadow of thy wings,
As the apple tree among the trees of the wood, so is my beloved among the sons. I sat down under his shadow with great delight, and his fruit was sweet to my taste.

Then said the Lord unto me, Go yet, love a woman beloved of her friend, yet an adulteress, according to the love of the Lord toward the children of Israel, who look to other gods, and love flagons of wine.
Ye have lived in pleasure on the earth, and been wanton; ye have nourished your hearts, as in a day of slaughter.
His left hand is under my head, and his right hand doth embrace me.
His left hand should be under my head, and his right hand should embrace me.
Surely against me is he turned; he turneth his hand against me all the day.
And my heart hath been secretly enticed, or my mouth hath kissed my hand:
If he be an angel he will do so, and you will feel his hand.

As he said this he held his hands palm upward for me to look at, and in
the centre of each was a small faint red spot.
I charge you, O ye daughters of Jerusalem, by the roes, and by the hinds of the field, that ye stir not up, nor awake my love, till he please.
I charge you, O daughters of Jerusalem, that ye stir not up, nor awake my love, until he please.
I charge you, O daughters of Jerusalem, if ye find my beloved, that ye tell him, that I am sick of love.
¶ Sing and rejoice, O daughter of Zion: for, lo, I come, and I will dwell in the midst of thee, saith the Lord.
I charge thee therefore before God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, who shall judge the quick and the dead at his appearing and his kingdom;
¶ The voice of my beloved! behold, he cometh leaping upon the mountains, skipping upon the hills.
Yea, behold at his voice do the hills and the mountains tremble and quake.
Why leap ye, ye high hills? this is the hill which God desireth to dwell in; yea, the Lord will dwell in it for ever.
Hear ye now what the Lord saith; Arise, contend thou before the mountains, and let the hills hear thy voice.
For, behold, the Lord cometh forth out of his place, and will come down, and tread upon the high places of the earth.
To him that rideth upon the heavens of heavens, which were of old; lo, he doth send out his voice, and that a mighty voice.
My beloved is like a roe or a young hart: behold, he standeth behind our wall, he looketh forth at the windows, shewing himself through the lattice.
Whither is thy beloved gone, O thou fairest among women? whither is thy beloved turned aside? that we may seek him with thee.
¶ I am my beloved’s, and his desire is toward me.
¶ Make haste, my beloved, and be thou like to a roe or to a young hart upon the mountains of spices.
My beloved spake, and said unto me, Rise up, my love, my fair one, and come away.
That the Lord spake unto me, saying,
And the Lord spake unto me, saying,
¶ The Lord spake also unto me again, saying,
The Lord spake also unto me again, saying:
The Spirit of the Lord spake by me, and his word was in my tongue.
For, lo, the winter is past, the rain is over and gone;
The flowers appear on the earth; the time of the singing of birds is come, and the voice of the turtle is heard in our land;
The whole earth is at rest, and is quiet; they break forth into singing.
The whole earth is at rest, and is quiet: they break forth into singing.
Thou that dwellest in the gardens, the companions hearken to thy voice: cause me to hear it.
The fig tree putteth forth her green figs, and the vines with the tender grape give a good smell. Arise, my love, my fair one, and come away.
Ye look and behold the fig trees, and ye see them with your eyes, and ye say when they begin to shoot forth, and their leaves are yet tender, that summer is now nigh at hand;
Now learn a parable of the fig tree—When its branches are yet tender, and it begins to put forth leaves, you know that summer is nigh at hand;
Now learn a parable of the fig tree; When her branch is yet tender, and putteth forth leaves, ye know that summer is near:
Whoso keepeth the fig tree shall eat the fruit thereof: so he that waiteth on his master shall be honoured.
¶ O my dove, that art in the clefts of the rock, in the secret places of the stairs, let me see thy countenance, let me hear thy voice; for sweet is thy voice, and thy countenance is comely.
Canst thou lift up thy voice to the clouds, that abundance of waters may cover thee?
Now, my God, let, I beseech thee, thine eyes be open, and let thine ears be attent unto the prayer that is made in this place.
Take us the foxes, the little foxes, that spoil the vines: for our vines have tender grapes.

Behold, I knew that all the fruit of the vineyard, save it were these, had become corrupted. And now these which have once brought forth good fruit have also become corrupted; and now all the trees of my vineyard are good for nothing save it be to be hewn down and cast into the fire.
Let us get up early to the vineyards; let us see if the vine flourish, whether the tender grape appear, and the pomegranates bud forth: there will I give thee my loves.
They shall fall by the sword: they shall be a portion for foxes.
Then said the trees unto the vine, Come thou, and reign over us.
¶ My beloved is mine, and I am his: he feedeth among the lilies.
My beloved is gone down into his garden, to the beds of spices, to feed in the gardens, and to gather lilies.

I am come into my garden, my sister, my spouse: I have gathered my myrrh with my spice; I have eaten my honeycomb with my honey; I have drunk my wine with my milk: eat, O friends; drink, yea, drink abundantly, O beloved.
My beloved is like a roe or a young hart: behold, he standeth behind our wall, he looketh forth at the windows, shewing himself through the lattice.
Until the day break, and the shadows flee away, turn, my beloved, and be thou like a roe or a young hart upon the mountains of Bether.
Until the day break, and the shadows flee away, I will get me to the mountain of myrrh, and to the hill of frankincense.
